title: Baccharis multibracteata
intro: Baccharis multibracteata, also known as huacarí, is a shrub-like plant native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. Its leaves and stems have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which help to treat conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Baccharis multibracteata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Baccharis multibracteata is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical applications such as creams and ointments, to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Baccharis multibracteata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It belongs to the genus Baccharis, which is a group of shrubby plants native to the Americas. Within the genus Baccharis, B. multibracteata is classified as a member of the tribe Mutisieae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. The exact classification of B. multibracteata is further specified at the species level.

parts: Baccharis multibracteata, also known as the Bolivian paperflower, is a plant used in traditional medicine.
